title: Ctenitis refulgens
taxonomy: Ctenitis refulgens is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of deciduous fern native to East Asia. The classification of Ctenitis refulgens is as follows: genus Ctenitis, family Dryopteridaceae, order Polypodiales, class Polypodiopsida, and phylum Pteridophyta. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that are commonly known as the wood ferns. These ferns are characterized by their woody rhizomes and often deciduous fronds.
uses: Ctenitis refulgens is reportedly used in traditional medicine, and its uses include treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin problems. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In folk medicine, Ctenitis refulgens is said to be used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis and asthma. Its leaves and stems are often used in the preparation of teas, infusions, and other remedies. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Ctenitis refulgens for medicinal purposes.
